title: Israeli Central Bank Slams Move to Label Top Banks an Oligopoly
summary: Israel’s central bank accused the competition watchdog of making an “extreme and disproportionate” decision following a move to categorize the country’s top lenders as an oligopoly.

title: Downing Street Braces for Tough Local London Elections
summary: No. 10 fears a capital crisis - will the Greens get right to the heart of Labour?

title: Supreme Court for Now Restores Abortion Pill Access
summary: The US Supreme Court temporarily restored the ability of patients to get a widely used abortion pill by mail, pausing a ruling that required people to visit a healthcare provider.

title: Bloomberg Talks: Jamieson Greer (Podcast)
summary: Source: Bloomberg, 11:23

title: Gold, Silver Surge as US-Iran Truce Hopes Ease Inflation Risks
summary: Gold and silver surged the most in about a month as hopes of a US-Iran deal drove oil prices lower and eased inflation expectations.

title: Iran War Optimism Propels Emerging-Market Stocks to Record High
summary: Emerging-market stocks rallied to an all-time high and a currency index rose to pre-war levels as traders grew optimistic that the US and Iran are moving closer to a peace deal.

title: GameStop Makes $56B Bid For eBay, M&A Animal Spirits Are Strong At Milken | Bloomberg Deals 5/6/2026
summary: A weekly, midday program that delivers high-impact, editorially driven coverage of the most important corporate transactions shaping the global market. Today's guests: Searchlight Capital Founding Partner Eric Zinterhofer, OakTree Capital Co-Portfolio Manager, Global Credit Danielle Poli, Octagon Credit Investors CEO Gretchen Lam, and General Atlantic Co-President & Head of Global Growth Equity Martin Escobari. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Dollar Hits Lowest Since War Started on Report End Is Near
summary: The dollar wraps up Wednesday at the weakest level since February as investors are hopeful of the imminent end to the US-Iran war.

title: Ellevest CEO: Great Wealth Transfer Already Underway
summary: Ellevest CEO Sylvia Kwan discusses ‘The Great Wealth Transfer’ and how the feminization of wealth will have the ripple effects throughout the economy. She talks with Katie Greifeld and Romaine Bostick at the Milken Institute Global Conference in Beverly Hills, California. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Eli Lilly Sells $9 Billion of Bonds to Fund Acquisitions
summary: Eli Lilly & Co. sold $9 billion of investment-grade bonds Wednesday — the company’s biggest-ever issuance — as it looks to finance an acquisition spree.

title: Canada’s Carbon Tax Hinders Pipeline Plans, Cenovus CEO Says
summary: Alberta’s planned oil pipeline to the west coast requires Canada to shift away from stricter climate policies and toward promoting greater oil production from new projects, Cenovus Energy Inc.’s chief executive officer said.

title: Worried About Public Market Depth for IPOs: Zinterhofer
summary:
Eric Zinterhofer, founding partner of Searchlight Capital, joins Scarlet Fu on "Bloomberg Deals." Wall Street banks are getting ready to raise billions of dollars taking data center companies public, even after IPO investors have already piled into anything that looks like a bet on artificial intelligence spending.
 (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Women's Sports Investment an 'Undervalued Asset,' Says Gold Medal Skier Breezy Johnson
summary: Olympic gold medal skier Breezy Johnson and Trisha Worthington, chief revenue and philanthropic officer for US Ski & Snowboard, said that sports sponsorships are a 'changing landscape' as NIL opportunities for college athletes and social media expand the scope of moneymaking opportunities for athletes.  Johnson said that despite record valuation, women's sports are still an 'undervalued asset' for investment and can lead to a greater return.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Vitol Is Turning to Mexico for Oil as War Disrupts Crude Market
summary: Vitol Group is buying Mexican oil for the first time in a decade as the conflict in the Middle East roils global supply chains.

title: MetLife’s Improved PE Returns Lift Earnings Above Expectations
summary: MetLife Inc.’s first-quarter earnings surpassed Wall Street expectations, as the insurer continued to cash in higher gains from its private equity portfolio.

title: Ted Turner Was 'A Legend': Eric Zinterhofer
summary: Eric Zinterhofer, founding partner of Searchlight Capital, joins Scarlet Fu on "Bloomberg Deals." Ted Turner, the swashbuckling billionaire who founded CNN, the first 24-hour news channel in the world, and turned his company into one of the biggest US cable-television systems before selling it to Time Warner Inc., has died. He was 87 (Source: Bloomberg)

title: FanDuel Names New Top Executive Amid Prediction Markets Push
summary: Flutter Entertainment Plc. elevated FanDuel President Christian Genetski to lead its US betting business, replacing Amy Howe, who served as chief executive officer of FanDuel for more than five years.

title: Stocks Hit All-Time Highs on Hopes War Is Near End: Markets Wrap
summary: Stocks climbed around the world, joining gains in bonds as oil retreated on hopes the US and Iran are nearing a deal to end a war that has jolted markets and clouded the economic outlook.

title: Yen Spikes to 10-Week High and Sparks Intervention Chatter
summary: Sharp moves in the Japanese yen that sent the currency to a 10-week high renewed speculation that the country is intervening in the market.

title: Canada May ‘Redeploy’ Capital Tied up in Airports, Carney Says
summary: Prime Minister Mark Carney said his government is considering spinning off or selling ownership stakes in Canadian airports in order to use the proceeds on new projects aimed at fueling economic growth.

title: Elite M&A Lawyers Fed Massive Insider-Trading Ring, US Alleges
summary: Lawyers from top mergers and acquisitions firms provided tips on some of biggest deals of the last decade to an insider trading ring that made tens of millions of dollars in illegal profits, federal prosecutors said.

title: Gundlach Warns Investors Will Lose Money on Private Credit
summary: DoubleLine Capital Chief Executive Officer Jeffrey Gundlach raised pointed questions about financial advisers and other intermediaries who ushered individual investors into private credit and other so-called semi-liquid funds, suggesting they’ve been motivated by high fees as much as by their clients’ interests.

title: Fortress Vice Chair Sloan on Maturity Wall Opportunities
summary: Fortress Vice Chairman & Global Head of Real Estate Tim Sloan discusses the impending commercial real estate maturity wall, and where he is looking for opportunities in the rest of the real estate market. He talks with Katie Greifeld and Romaine Bostick at the Milken Institute Global Conference in Beverly Hills, California. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Treasury Repo Trading Debate Spurs Big Wagers on Lending Rates
summary: A discussion around the prospect of US Treasury investing its excess cash into short-term money markets unleashed a flurry of wagers betting on spread movements between overnight lending rates.

title: Oil and Gas Plunge as Iran Weighs New US Proposal to End War
summary: Oil and natural gas fell sharply as Iran evaluated a new proposal from the US to end the war that has lasted nearly 10 weeks.

title: Foreign Demand for Treasury Debt Is Stalling, Trade Group Says
summary: Foreign investors are showing signs of diversifying away from US Treasuries as debt levels mount, according to the financial industry’s global trade group.

title: Brewers Owner Has No Plans to Sell Stake to PE
summary: Milwaukee Brewers Chair & Principal Owner Mark Attanasio talks about revenue sharing, ongoing labor negotiations, his vision for the team and says he doesn't ever expect to sell a stake in the team to private equity. Attanasio also talks about owning a pro soccer team in Europe. He talks with Katie Greifeld and Romaine Bostick at the Milken Institute Global Conference in Beverly Hills, California. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Bruin Capital Invests in the Tech Behind Sports, CEO Says
summary: Bruin Capital Founder & CEO George Pyne discusses investing in ‘second level enablers,’ the state of sports and entertainment media, and why globalization is the future. He talks with Katie Greifeld and Romaine Bostick at the Milken Institute Global Conference in Beverly Hills, California. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: M&A Animal Spirits Are Strong at Milken
summary: Bloomberg's Matt Monks joins Scarlet Fu on "Bloomberg Deals." They discuss dealmakers at the Milken Institute Global Conference, one of the most influential gatherings in global finance and policy, drawing a who’s who of CEOs, founders, financiers, politicians and celebrities. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Searchlight Capital’s Zinterhofer Sees Bifurcated M&A Market
summary: Searchlight Capital Partners founding partner Eric Zinterhofer said he thinks dealmaking has bifurcated, with multiple buyers competing for good assets at high prices while less-viable companies are kept off the market.

title: Fed's Goolsbee Warns Against Reflexively Lowering Rates
summary: Federal Reserve Bank of Chicago President Austan Goolsbee warns against reflexively lowering interest rates in response to faster productivity growth, as it can sometimes drive up inflation. He speaks at the Milken Institute Global Conference in Beverly Hills, California. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Congo’s Biggest Lender Rawbank Posts 9% Rise in Annual Profit
summary: Rawbank SA, the biggest lender in the Democratic Republic of Congo, saw after-tax profit increase at 9% to $232 million in 2025.

title: SpaceX IPO 'Will Be Volatile' Says Ark Invest's Cathie Wood
summary: Ark Invest CEO and CIO Cathie Wood joined Bloomberg's Carol Massar and Joe Mathieu at the Milken Institute Global Conference in Beverley Hills, California. She   talks about a potential IPO for Elon Musk's SpaceX, Musk investing more in chips, the need for data centers and the growth of AI stocks. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: CVS Results Beat, Extending Healthcare Win Streak
summary: CVS raised its earnings outlook for the year after profit and revenue in the first quarter exceeded analyst expectations, the latest in a string of positive reports from US health conglomerates. Ike Swetlitz reports on Bloomberg Open Interest. (Source: Bloomberg)
